title: Add native_sim L2 loopback packet-path test
description: Why this exists: the active Zephyr module now builds and boots LICHEN_L2 on native_sim with the loopback LoRa driver, but we still need an automated packet-path proof. What needs to be done: add a native_sim or ztest harness that sends an IPv6/UDP/CoAP packet through the LICHEN_L2 TX path, loops it through the LoRa loopback driver, verifies LICHEN link frame handling/SCHC decompression on RX, and confirms the packet reaches the Zephyr IPv6 stack or a test observer. Acceptance criteria: the test runs in CI/Linux with CONFIG_LICHEN_L2=y, fails if framing/compression/RX injection is bypassed, and documents any native_sim TAP/zeth requirements.
notes: Implementation completed for native_sim L2 packet-path gate. Replaced dummy-L2 ping_l2 test with CONFIG_LICHEN_L2 + CONFIG_LORA_LOOPBACK path; test provisions deterministic signing/link keys through ZTEST-only hooks, selects the LICHEN_L2 interface explicitly, sends a checksum-correct IPv6/ICMPv6 packet through L2 send, drains stale loopback queue before baseline, and asserts TX counters, LoRa loopback send/recv counters, RX injection counter, plus exact injected IPv6 bytes captured after net_recv_data(). Added loopback ztest counters, L2 ztest key/stats/capture hooks, local lichen vendor prefix, native_sim overlay, and fixed L2 send to return byte count per Zephyr net_l2 contract. Verification on EC2 builder i-0b363aaa46f90a38f with EBS vol-0875d0df8bdf82a58: west build -p always -b native_sim -d build/ping_l2-native lichen/tests/ping_l2 && west build -d build/ping_l2-native -t run PASSED; west twister -T lichen/tests/ping_l2 -p native_sim --inline-logs --verbose PASSED 1/1. Local macOS cannot run native_sim POSIX target. Code review passes: initial 3 reviewers found L2 send zero-byte return, counter-only false-positive risk, loopback queue drain risk; all fixed. Final 3 reviewers found doc/comment drift, loopback MTU fidelity, size_t assert native_sim risk, and test scope/naming; fixed doc/comment, set test loopback MTU=255, relaxed assertion to bounded payload requirement, renamed comments to packet-path. Remaining P2 scope note: test proves LICHEN_L2 -> LoRa loopback -> RX callback -> net_recv_data injection, not post-IP ICMP/UDP/CoAP app delivery; follow-up filed as project-LICHEN-ev5b.12.
close_reason: Completed: native_sim L2 packet-path gate implemented, EC2 west build/run and twister pass, three independent review passes recorded with fixes, follow-up project-LICHEN-ev5b.12 filed for post-IP UDP/CoAP observer.
